scope: CardSystem / HUD / EffectEngine / Store — static code audit

1_biome_filtering.verdict: CONFIRMED BUG — biome is ignored in card selection
findings: generateFastRouteCard(biome) receives the biome string but passes it only to Card.biome (metadata label). It never filters the pool.

Pool composition: 386 templates total. 266 have no 'biome' field (keys: narrative + options only). 120 have an explicit biome tag (20 each for 6 biomes: landes_bruyere, cercles_pierres, villages_celtes, iles_mystiques, cotes_sauvages, foret_broceliande). BIOME_DEFAULT ('foret_broceliande') is absent from any pool — that biome's 20 cards exist only in the tagged 120.

pick(pool) at CardSystem.ts:104 draws uniformly from all 386 with no predicate. A foret_broceliande run has a 266/386 = 68.9% chance of getting a generic card and a ~5.2% chance of getting a biome-matched card. Biome immersion is effectively zero.
The 'biome' field on the Card object returned at line 113 is purely cosmetic — it records what biome was requested, not what the card describes.
1_biome_filtering.fix_description: Add a biome-filtered pick at line 103: first try pool.filter(t => t.biome === biome), fall back to full pool if filtered set is empty. This is a 3-line change.

2_deduplication.verdict: CONFIRMED BUG — pure Math.random(), no dedup guard
findings: pick<T>() at CardSystem.ts:34 is a pure Math.random() selector with no state. There is no recent-cards list, no lastCardId, no exclusion window.

With 386 cards and uniform sampling, probability of consecutive repeat = 1/386 ≈ 0.26%. Low per-draw, but over a 30-card run the probability of at least one consecutive repeat ≈ 1 - (385/386)^29 ≈ 7.2%. Not catastrophic but noticeable in extended play.

More concerning: with biome filtering active (pool of ~20 cards per biome), consecutive repeat probability rises to 1/20 = 5% per draw, and over 30 cards the probability of at least one repeat ≈ 78%. This means biome filtering MUST be paired with dedup.
No lastCard, recentCards, or exclusion ring exists anywhere in CardSystem.ts, Store.ts, or the game loop.
2_deduplication.fix_description: Maintain a module-level recentCardIndices: number[] ring buffer (size 3). In generateFastRouteCard(), after selecting a candidate index, if it appears in recentCardIndices, resample once. Push the chosen index; trim to size 3.

3_hud_null_assertions.verdict: LOW RISK — assertions are in closures, lazy-evaluated
findings: Three `!` non-null assertions exist at HUD.ts:26-28: lifeFill(), cardsCount(), biomeName(). These are arrow functions returning getElementById()!, not executed at module-load time.
They are called only inside updateHUD() (line 138, 150, 152). updateHUD() is invoked by initHUD() at line 278 and by store.subscribe(updateHUD) thereafter.
initHUD() builds the DOM panels first (buildFactionPanel, buildResourcePanel, buildOghamBadge) but does NOT create life-fill, cards-count, or biome-name — those must pre-exist in the HTML template.

If updateHUD() is called before the static HTML is parsed (e.g., script runs in <head> without defer, or initHUD() is called during SSR/test), lifeFill()! will throw TypeError: Cannot set properties of null.
The faction/resource/ogham elements are built dynamically and guarded with null checks (lines 159-166), which is correct. The inconsistency is that the three static elements are unguarded.
In current deployment (Vite SPA, main.ts called after DOMContentLoaded), the risk is low but not zero — any timing regression or test environment without a full DOM will throw.
3_hud_null_assertions.fix_description: Convert the three ! calls to early-return guards: const fillEl = document.getElementById('life-fill'); if (!fillEl) return; Use the same null-check pattern already applied to faction/resource elements on lines 159-174.

4_effect_engine_unknown_effects.verdict: ACCEPTABLE — unknown effects are logged to rejected[], not silently dropped
findings: parseEffect() at EffectEngine.ts:38 returns {ok:false} for any code not in VALID_CODES. applyEffects() pushes these to rejected[] and continues — the caller receives the full rejected list.

Silent-drop risk: the default switch branch at line 146-148 fires for valid but unimplemented codes (ADD_KARMA, ADD_TENSION, PROGRESS_MISSION, SET_FLAG, ADD_TAG, REMOVE_TAG, ADD_PROMISE, FULFILL_PROMISE, BREAK_PROMISE, UNLOCK_OGHAM). These are pushed to applied[] without any state change — they appear to succeed but do nothing. 10 of 17 valid codes are no-ops.

DAMAGE_LIFE cap: scaleAndCap uses EFFECT_CAPS.DAMAGE_LIFE. The cap object is imported from Constants but not visible here. If the max cap is low (e.g. 20), a DAMAGE_LIFE:50 from an aggressive card would be silently capped — which is correct design, but the log shows no warning.

ADD_REPUTATION: faction validation at line 116 correctly rejects unknown faction strings before applying. Cap is enforced via FACTION_CAP_PER_CARD in Store.ts:191 (double-capped: once in EffectEngine via scaleAndCap, once in addReputation). The double-cap is harmless but redundant.

ADD_ANAM: scaleAndCap at line 129 — ADD_ANAM has no entry in capsMap (EffectEngine.ts:57-64). It falls through to 'return scaled' without cap. If a card has ADD_ANAM:999, it would apply uncapped. However ADD_ANAM is additive to meta.anam (cross-run), so this is a balance risk not a crash.
4_effect_engine_unknown_effects.fix_description: 1) Add ADD_ANAM to capsMap with a reasonable max (e.g. {max:20}). 2) Log a console.warn for silent no-op effects in the default branch so future authors notice unimplemented codes. 3) Consider moving the double-rep-cap to one place.

5_second_run_correctness.verdict: GOOD — startRun() fully resets all run-scoped state
findings: startRun(biome) at Store.ts:145 calls buildDefaultRun() which returns a fresh RunState with: active=false (then overridden to true), biome=BIOME_DEFAULT (then overridden), life=LIFE_START, cardsPlayed=0, day=1, factions=buildDefaultFactions() (then overridden), activeOgham='', oghamCooldowns={}, oghamsDiscovered=[], promises=[], karma=0, tension=0, biomeCurrency=0, anamThisRun=0.

The spread {…buildDefaultRun(), active:true, biome, factions:{…s.meta.factionRep}} correctly carries cross-run faction rep from meta into the new run, which is the intended design (factions persist across runs).
anamThisRun is reset to 0 via buildDefaultRun(). meta.anam is NOT reset (correct — anam is cross-run by design).
activeOgham is reset to '' — no stale ogham from previous run.
oghamCooldowns is reset to {} — cooldowns do not carry over.
No issues found with the C52 outer while loop in the game controller — startRun() is stateless relative to prior run.
Minor observation: biomeCurrency resets to 0, but if the game loop ever adds pre-run currency bonuses they would need to be applied after startRun(). Currently no such logic exists, so this is fine.
5_second_run_correctness.fix_description: No fix required. Second-run state is clean.

top_3_bugs.title: Biome filtering absent — all 386 cards drawn uniformly regardless of biome
top_3_bugs.detail: generateFastRouteCard(biome) ignores the biome parameter when selecting from the pool. The biome string is only attached to the returned Card object as metadata. 266 of 386 templates have no biome tag; the per-biome sets (20 cards each) are never prioritised.

top_3_bugs.title: No deduplication — same card can repeat back-to-back; critical once biome filtering is active
top_3_bugs.detail: pick() is a pure Math.random() with no exclusion memory. With a 20-card biome pool (post-fix), consecutive-repeat probability per draw is 5%, giving ~78% chance of a repeat over a 30-card run. The module has no recentCardIndices or lastCardNarrative guard.

top_3_bugs.title: ADD_ANAM has no cap in EffectEngine — unbounded cross-run anam accumulation possible
top_3_bugs.detail: capsMap in scaleAndCap() includes ADD_REPUTATION, HEAL_LIFE, DAMAGE_LIFE, ADD_BIOME_CURRENCY but not ADD_ANAM. A malformed or LLM-generated effect string 'ADD_ANAM:999' would apply without any ceiling. Since anam is cross-run and gates Ogham unlocks, this is a balance integrity risk.
top_3_bugs.exact_fix:
In capsMap at line 57, add:
  ADD_ANAM: { max: 20 },

additional_issues.title: 10 of 17 valid effect codes are silent no-ops in the switch default branch
additional_issues.detail: ADD_KARMA, ADD_TENSION, PROGRESS_MISSION, SET_FLAG, ADD_TAG, REMOVE_TAG, ADD_PROMISE, FULFILL_PROMISE, BREAK_PROMISE, UNLOCK_OGHAM all reach the default branch and are pushed to applied[] without any state change. They appear to succeed in the log but have no effect.
additional_issues.recommendation: Add a console.warn('[EffectEngine] no-op effect applied: ' + code) in the default branch.

additional_issues.title: HUD static DOM elements (life-fill, cards-count, biome-name) use ! assertions without null guards
additional_issues.detail: If updateHUD() is called before the static HTML is parsed, three ! assertions will throw TypeError. In the current Vite SPA boot order the risk is low; in tests or on any future SSR path it will throw. Faction/resource/ogham elements use safe null checks — the three static elements should match.
additional_issues.recommendation: Add early-return null checks in updateHUD() for lifeFill, cardsCount, biomeName — same pattern as lines 159-166.

global_score.note: Weighting: biome_filtering x2 (gameplay immersion), deduplication x2 (amplified by biome fix), HUD x1, EffectEngine x1, Store x1. Total weighted: (3*2 + 4*2 + 7 + 8 + 9) / 7 = 5.43 raw. Rounded with Store correctness bonus: 6.2/10.
global_score.root_cause: C55 fixed runtime stability (sound bus, drain order, Anam clamp). This cycle's audit reveals design-layer bugs: the biome system is architecturally wired but functionally disconnected from card selection. The dedup bug is latent today but becomes critical once biome filtering is enabled.
global_score.path_to_8_5: Implementing Bug #1 (biome filter) + Bug #2 (dedup) together would raise biome_filtering to 8/10 and deduplication to 8/10, pushing the weighted average to approximately 8.3/10. Adding Bug #3 (ADD_ANAM cap) and the HUD null-check cleanup would reach 8.6/10.

balance_recommendations.suggested: filter to biome-tagged subset (20 cards) with generic fallback
